description_long: This sconce features a dome-shaped frosted glass cover that diffuses light, mounted on a wide, circular metallic base with a bronze or copper finish. The base is slightly flared and has a glossy, reflective surface, giving it a classic yet elegant appearance. The rear of the fixture reveals electrical connectors and mounting hardware, indicating it is designed to be affixed to a wall or ceiling. The glass dome is smooth and opaque, providing a soft glow when illuminated. The metallic base is robust and likely made of steel or brass, with a dark, polished coating. There are no visible decorative motifs, making the design simple and functional, with a slight vintage or industrial influence.
description: A dome-shaped frosted glass sconce with a wide, glossy metallic base and exposed electrical connectors, blending classic and industrial design elements.
description_view_0: Side view showing the frosted glass dome and the flared, reflective metallic base.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the electrical connectors and mounting hardware on the back of the base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the full length of the sconce and the smooth transition from the base to the glass dome.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the frosted glass dome and the circular metallic rim surrounding it.
attribute_fixture_design: The fixture has a vintage-industrial design, with a glossy bronze metallic base and a simple frosted glass dome. Its clean lines and lack of ornate decoration give it a utilitarian yet classic appearance.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
